build.signature: (def_name: 'str' = 'scitex-cloud-shared-v0.1.0', output_dir: 'str | Path | None' = None, force: 'bool' = False, sandbox: 'bool' = False, *, def_path: 'str | Path | None' = None, image_name: 'str | None' = None, use_sudo: 'bool' = False, fakeroot: 'bool | None' = None) -> 'Path'
build.doc: Build Apptainer/Singularity SIF or sandbox from .def file.

build_host_mount_binds.doc: Build ``--bind`` argument pairs for host package mounts.

build_instance_start_script.doc: Build a bash script that starts an apptainer instance and keeps it alive.

build_reproducible.doc: Run the reproducible round-trip and manage the artifact store.

build_sbatch_command.doc: Build ``sbatch`` command to submit an allocation script.

capture_lock.doc: Introspect a built SIF and write a combined ``.lock`` file.

check_verified.doc: Check a built image's reproducibility marker — NOISY on every use.

cleanup.doc: Remove old scitex-v*.sif files, keeping the N most recent.

cleanup_sandboxes.doc: Remove old sandbox directories, keeping the N most recent.

cleanup_sifs.doc: Remove SIF files and related artifacts.

compare_locks.doc: Compare two locks' flat version sets — the round-trip gate.

deploy.doc: Copy active SIF and base SIF to target directory.

freeze.signature: (sif_path: 'str | Path', output_dir: 'str | Path | None' = None) -> 'dict[str, Path]'
freeze.doc: Extract pinned versions from a built SIF.

generate_locked_def.doc: Emit a version-pinned ``.def`` from a rough ``.def`` + a lock.

get_active_sandbox.doc: Read current-sandbox symlink to determine active sandbox version.

get_active_version.doc: Read current.sif symlink to determine active version.

is_sandbox.doc: Check if path is a sandbox directory (not a SIF image).

list_builds.doc: List the timestamped builds of ``layer`` under ``root``.

list_sandboxes.doc: List all versioned sandbox directories with metadata.

list_versions.doc: List all versioned SIFs with metadata.

mark_unverified.doc: Write the ``.unverified`` marker for a build (clears ``.verified``).

mark_verified.doc: Write the ``.verified`` marker for a build (clears ``.unverified``).

point_latest.doc: Point the layer ``latest`` symlink at the ``(layer, ts)`` build.

protect.doc: Write the ``.keep`` prune-protect marker for a build.

prune.doc: Prune old builds of ``layer``, keeping the ``retain`` most recent.

read_lock.doc: Parse a combined ``.lock`` file back into a ``Lock``.

rollback.doc: Switch to the version before the current one (by modification time).

rollback_sandbox.doc: Switch to the sandbox before the current one (by modification time).

sandbox_configure_ps1.doc: Set PS1 prompt in a sandbox's environment script.

sandbox_create.doc: Build a sandbox directory from a SIF image or .def file.

sandbox_maintain.doc: Run a command inside a sandbox with --writable --fakeroot flags.

sandbox_to_sif.doc: Convert a sandbox directory back to a SIF image.

sandbox_update.signature: (sandbox_dir: 'str | Path', *, proj_root: 'str | Path | None' = None, packages: 'tuple[str, ...] | None' = None, install_deps: 'bool' = False) -> 'dict[str, str]'
sandbox_update.doc: Incrementally update ecosystem packages inside an existing sandbox.

status.doc: List available containers and their status.

switch_sandbox.doc: Atomically switch current-sandbox symlink to sandbox-{version}/.

switch_version.doc: Atomically switch current.sif symlink to scitex-v{version}.sif.

verify_roundtrip.doc: Rebuild from the locked def, compare version sets, mark the build.

write_lock.doc: Serialize a ``Lock`` to the combined ``.lock`` text format.

get_dev_mounts.doc: Generate Docker volume mount strings for development repositories.

rebuild.doc: Rebuild Docker containers without using the cache.

status.doc: Get Docker container status for the given compose environment.

check_packages.doc: Check which host packages are installed.

get_mount_config.doc: Parse mount configuration and return structured bind mount info.

get_texlive_binds.doc: Generate bind mount entries for TeXLive.

install_packages.doc: Install host packages by calling the shell script.
